---
title: "all gender graphs"
format: docx
date: today
editor: visual
execute:
  enabled: true
df-print: kable
project: 
  execute-dir: project
---

## Male-only Models Graphs

Load Data

```{r, include=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(knitr)
library(broom)
library(gt)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

```

```{r}

roi_fa_allgender <-  read_csv(here("data", "raw", "rawdata_manuscript_FA.csv")) |>
  rename(
    sus  = `Total Drug Use`
  ) |>
  filter(
    !is.na(pclr),
    !is.na(FA_mean)
  )

roi_fa_allgender$gender <- factor(roi_fa_allgender$gender) 


roi_fa_allgender <- roi_fa_allgender %>%
  mutate(
    gender = factor(gender,
                    levels = c(0, 1),
                    labels = c("Male", "Female"))
  )

roi_fa_allgender <- roi_fa_allgender %>%
  mutate(
    roi = recode(roi,
      "JHU_CgC_L" = "Left DC",
      "JHU_CgC_R" = "Right DC",
      "JHU_UF_L"  = "Left UF",
      "JHU_UF_R"  = "Right UF"
    )
  )
```


## Model 1
Partial Effects Plot
```{r}
plots_m1_all <- roi_fa_allgender %>%
  group_by(roi) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    # Fit Model 1
    fit = map(data, ~ lm(FA_mean ~ pclr + age + gender, data = .x)),

    # Prediction grid: pclr 0–40, covariates at ROI means,
    # predictions generated for BOTH genders
    pred = map2(data, fit, ~ {

      age_mean <- mean(.x$age, na.rm = TRUE)
     
      genders <- levels(.x$gender)

      newdat <- expand.grid(
        pclr   = seq(0, 40, by = 1),
        gender = genders
      ) %>%
        as_tibble() %>%
        mutate(
          age = age_mean,
        )

      p <- predict(.y, newdata = newdat, se.fit = TRUE)

      newdat %>%
        mutate(
          fit = as.numeric(p$fit),
          se  = as.numeric(p$se.fit),
          lo  = fit - 1.96 * se,
          hi  = fit + 1.96 * se
        )
    }),

    plot = pmap(list(data, pred, roi), ~
      ggplot() +
        geom_point(data = ..1, aes(x = pclr, y = FA_mean), alpha = 0.5) +
        geom_ribbon(data = ..2,
                    aes(x = pclr, ymin = lo, ymax = hi, fill = gender),
                    alpha = 0.15) +
        geom_line(data = ..2,
                  aes(x = pclr, y = fit, color = gender),
                  linewidth = 1.1) +
        coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 40), ylim = c(0.35, 0.6)) +
        labs(
          title = paste0("Model 1 (age, gender controlled): ", ..3),
          subtitle = "Predicted FA across PCL-R for each gender",
          x = "PCL-R Total Score",
          y = "Mean Fractional Anisotropy (FA)",
          color = "Gender",
          fill  = "Gender"
        ) +
        theme_minimal()
    )
  ) %>%
  pull(plot)

walk(plots_m1_all, print)
```

## Model 2
Partial Effects Plot
```{r}
plots_m2_all <- roi_fa_allgender %>%
  group_by(roi) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    # Fit Model 2
    fit = map(data, ~ lm(FA_mean ~ pclr + age + iq + sus + gender, data = .x)),

    # Prediction grid: pclr 0–40, covariates held at ROI means
    # gender held at reference level
    pred = map2(data, fit, ~ {

      age_mean <- mean(.x$age, na.rm = TRUE)
      iq_mean  <- mean(.x$iq,  na.rm = TRUE)
      sus_mean <- mean(.x$sus, na.rm = TRUE)
      ref_gender <- levels(.x$gender)[1]

      newdat <- tibble(
        pclr   = seq(0, 40, by = 1),
        age    = age_mean,
        iq     = iq_mean,
        sus    = sus_mean,
        gender = ref_gender
      )

      p <- predict(.y, newdata = newdat, se.fit = TRUE)

      newdat %>%
        mutate(
          fit = as.numeric(p$fit),
          se  = as.numeric(p$se.fit),
          lo  = fit - 1.96 * se,
          hi  = fit + 1.96 * se
        )
    }),

    plot = pmap(list(data, pred, roi), ~
      ggplot() +
        geom_point(data = ..1, aes(x = pclr, y = FA_mean), alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_ribbon(data = ..2,
                    aes(x = pclr, ymin = lo, ymax = hi),
                    alpha = 0.2) +
        geom_line(data = ..2,
                  aes(x = pclr, y = fit),
                  linewidth = 1) +
        coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 40), ylim = c(0.35, 0.6)) +
        labs(
          title = paste0("Model 2 (age, IQ, SUS, gender controlled): ", ..3),
          subtitle = paste(
            "Age, IQ, SUS held at ROI means | Gender =",
            levels(..1$gender)[1]
          ),
          x = "PCL-R Total Score",
          y = "Mean Fractional Anisotropy (FA)"
        ) +
        theme_minimal()
    )
  ) %>%
  pull(plot)

walk(plots_m2_all, print)
```

Two Prediction Lines
```{r}
plots_m2_all <- roi_fa_allgender %>%
  group_by(roi) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    # Fit Model 2
    fit = map(data, ~ lm(FA_mean ~ pclr + age + iq + sus + gender, data = .x)),

    # Prediction grid: pclr 0–40, covariates at ROI means,
    # predictions generated for BOTH genders
    pred = map2(data, fit, ~ {

      age_mean <- mean(.x$age, na.rm = TRUE)
      iq_mean  <- mean(.x$iq,  na.rm = TRUE)
      sus_mean <- mean(.x$sus, na.rm = TRUE)

      genders <- levels(.x$gender)

      newdat <- expand.grid(
        pclr   = seq(0, 40, by = 1),
        gender = genders
      ) %>%
        as_tibble() %>%
        mutate(
          age = age_mean,
          iq  = iq_mean,
          sus = sus_mean
        )

      p <- predict(.y, newdata = newdat, se.fit = TRUE)

      newdat %>%
        mutate(
          fit = as.numeric(p$fit),
          se  = as.numeric(p$se.fit),
          lo  = fit - 1.96 * se,
          hi  = fit + 1.96 * se
        )
    }),

    plot = pmap(list(data, pred, roi), ~
      ggplot() +
        geom_point(data = ..1, aes(x = pclr, y = FA_mean), alpha = 0.5) +
        geom_ribbon(data = ..2,
                    aes(x = pclr, ymin = lo, ymax = hi, fill = gender),
                    alpha = 0.15) +
        geom_line(data = ..2,
                  aes(x = pclr, y = fit, color = gender),
                  linewidth = 1.1) +
        coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 40), ylim = c(0.35, 0.6)) +
        labs(
          title = paste0("Model 2 (age, IQ, SUS controlled): ", ..3),
          subtitle = "Predicted FA across PCL-R for each gender",
          x = "PCL-R Total Score",
          y = "Mean Fractional Anisotropy (FA)",
          color = "Gender",
          fill  = "Gender"
        ) +
        theme_minimal()
    )
  ) %>%
  pull(plot)

walk(plots_m2_all, print)
```
